text: A banana is a yellow fruit that is soft and sweet.
A lemon is yellow and very sour.
A chili pepper is red and extremely spicy.
An apple can be red, green, or yellow and has a sweet taste.
Biryani is a flavorful Indian rice dish made with spices, rice, and usually meat or vegetables.
Samosa is a fried or baked pastry with a spicy potato filling, popular as a snack in India.
Paneer butter masala is a creamy tomato-based curry made with Indian cottage cheese.
Masala dosa is a thin, crispy fermented crepe filled with spicy mashed potatoes.
Chole is a spicy chickpea curry from North India, often eaten with rice or bhature.
Rasgulla is a spongy dessert made from ball-shaped dumplings of chhena cooked in light syrup.
Naan is a soft and fluffy flatbread that is traditionally baked in a tandoor.
Tandoori chicken is chicken marinated in yogurt and spices, roasted in a tandoor.
Gulab jamun is a deep-fried milk-solid ball soaked in sweet syrup, served as dessert.
Pav bhaji is a spicy vegetable mash served with soft buttered buns.
Raita is a yogurt-based condiment that cools down spicy dishes.
Dhokla is a soft and fluffy savory cake made from fermented rice and chickpea flour.
Butter chicken is a rich tomato gravy dish with tender pieces of marinated chicken.
Lassi is a cold yogurt drink blended with sugar, salt, or fruits like mango.
Momo is a type of South Asian dumpling popular in Nepal, filled with meat or vegetables and served with spicy chutney.
Sel roti is a traditional homemade, sweet, ring-shaped rice bread from Nepal.
Hilsa curry is a traditional Bengali dish made with hilsa fish and mustard gravy.
Bhuna khichuri is a spiced rice and lentil dish often served with beef curry in Bangladesh.
Nihari is a slow-cooked stew of beef or lamb, eaten as breakfast in Pakistan.
Chicken Karahi is a spicy curry cooked in a wok-like pan called a karahi, popular in Pakistan.
Pad Thai is a stir-fried noodle dish with shrimp, peanuts, and tamarind sauce, one of Thailand's national dishes.
Tom Yum is a hot and sour Thai soup made with shrimp, lemongrass, and lime juice.
Pho is a Vietnamese noodle soup consisting of broth, rice noodles, herbs, and meat.
Nasi Goreng is Indonesian-style fried rice often cooked with kecap manis, garlic, shallots, and chili.
Rendang is a spicy meat dish slow-cooked in coconut milk and spices, originating from Indonesia.
Nasi lemak is Malaysia's national dish consisting of fragrant rice cooked in coconut milk, served with sambal, anchovies, and egg.
Adobo is a Filipino dish made by marinating meat in vinegar, soy sauce, and garlic, then simmering it.
Halo-halo is a Filipino dessert made from crushed ice, evaporated milk, and mixed ingredients like sweet beans, fruits, and jellies.
Peking duck is a famous Beijing dish featuring crispy roasted duck served with pancakes, hoisin sauce, and scallions.
Kung Pao chicken is a spicy stir-fry dish made with chicken, peanuts, vegetables, and chili peppers.
Mapo tofu is a Sichuan dish featuring soft tofu in a spicy chili and bean-based sauce, typically with minced pork.
Xiao long bao are Chinese soup dumplings filled with meat and hot broth, typically served in a bamboo steamer.
Chow mein is a stir-fried noodle dish made with vegetables and sometimes meat or seafood.
Hot and sour soup is a Chinese soup made with mushrooms, tofu, bamboo shoots, and egg, flavored with vinegar and white pepper.
Dim sum refers to a variety of small Chinese dishes served with tea, including dumplings, buns, and rolls.
Mooncake is a traditional Chinese pastry filled with lotus seed paste or red bean, eaten during the Mid-Autumn Festival.
Kimchi is a traditional Korean side dish made from fermented vegetables, usually cabbage and radish, with chili pepper and garlic.
Bibimbap is a Korean mixed rice dish topped with sautéed vegetables, sliced meat, a fried egg, and chili paste.
Tteokbokki is a popular Korean street food made of chewy rice cakes in a spicy red sauce.
Beef noodle soup is a beloved Taiwanese dish made with braised beef, vegetables, and noodles in a flavorful broth.
Lu rou fan is a classic Taiwanese comfort food of minced pork belly cooked in soy sauce, served over rice.
Bubble tea is a Taiwanese drink made with tea, milk, and chewy tapioca pearls.
Char siu is a Cantonese-style barbecued pork known for its sweet and savory glaze.
Egg tarts are a popular Hong Kong dessert consisting of a flaky pastry crust filled with egg custard.
Wonton noodles are a Hong Kong dish made with thin egg noodles and wontons in a clear broth.
Meat pie is an iconic Australian dish consisting of a flaky pastry filled with minced meat and gravy.
Lamingtons are sponge cakes coated in chocolate and rolled in coconut, a popular Australian dessert.
Pavlova is a meringue-based dessert topped with fresh fruit and cream, considered a national dish of New Zealand.
Hangi is a traditional Māori method of cooking meat and vegetables in an underground oven.
Palusami is a Polynesian dish of taro leaves filled with coconut cream and sometimes meat, baked in banana leaves.
Oka is a Samoan raw fish salad marinated in citrus and coconut milk, similar to ceviche.
Lovo is a Fijian feast where food is wrapped in banana leaves and cooked in an earth oven.
Kokoda is a Fijian dish of raw fish marinated in lime juice and coconut milk, similar to ceviche.
Poke is a Hawaiian dish of diced raw fish, typically seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Spam musubi is a Hawaiian snack made of rice and Spam wrapped in seaweed.
Loco moco is a Hawaiian dish consisting of rice topped with a hamburger patty, fried egg, and brown gravy.
Sushi is a Japanese dish of vinegared rice combined with seafood, vegetables, and sometimes tropical fruits.
Ramen is a Japanese noodle soup dish with Chinese-style wheat noodles served in a meat or fish-based broth.
Tempura is a Japanese dish of seafood or vegetables that have been battered and deep-fried.
Miso soup is a traditional Japanese soup consisting of a stock called dashi into which softened miso paste is mixed.
Okonomiyaki is a savory Japanese pancake containing a variety of ingredients.
Hummus is a Middle Eastern dip or spread made from cooked, mashed chickpeas blended with tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, and garlic.
Falafel consists of deep-fried balls of ground chickpeas or fava beans, typically served in pita bread.
Shawarma is a Middle Eastern meat preparation where thin slices of lamb, chicken, or beef are stacked and roasted on a vertical rotisserie.
Tabbouleh is a Levantine vegetarian salad made of finely chopped parsley, tomatoes, mint, and bulgur.
Baklava is a rich, sweet dessert pastry made of layers of filo filled with chopped nuts and sweetened with syrup or honey.
Buuz are Mongolian steamed dumplings filled with minced meat, typically mutton or beef.
Khorkhog is a Mongolian barbecue dish where meat is cooked with hot stones inside a sealed container.
Airag is a traditional Mongolian alcoholic drink made from fermented mare's milk.
Boortsog are Mongolian deep-fried cookies often served with tea or used as a dessert.
Suutei tsai is a traditional Mongolian milk tea made with green tea, milk, water, and salt.

Halwa Puri is a beloved Pakistani breakfast dish that is extremely popular in Lahore and other cities of Punjab. It consists of deep-fried puri bread made from refined flour, served alongside sweet semolina halwa cooked in ghee and sugar, spiced chickpea curry known as chana, and a tangy aloo bhaji made with potatoes. This dish holds deep cultural significance in Pakistani households and is traditionally served on weekends and special occasions. It is a vegetarian dish and is commonly found at street stalls across Pakistan early in the morning.

Sajji is a traditional slow-roasted Pakistani dish originating from the Balochistan province. It consists of a whole lamb or chicken marinated minimally with salt and sometimes papaya paste as a tenderizer, then skewered on a large metal rod and roasted over an open wood fire for several hours. The meat becomes incredibly tender and smoky on the outside while remaining juicy inside. Sajji is considered a ceremonial dish often prepared for weddings, festivals, and large gatherings. It is halal, gluten-free, and high in protein.

Chapli Kebab is a signature Pakistani minced meat patty originating from Peshawar in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. It is made from minced beef or mutton mixed with tomatoes, green chilies, coriander seeds, pomegranate seeds, onions, and eggs, then flattened into wide round patties and shallow fried in beef tallow. The result is a crispy exterior with a juicy, spicy interior. Chapli Kebab is a staple street food across Pakistan and is typically served with naan bread, sliced onions, and fresh coriander chutney. It is rich in protein and iron.

Dum Pukht Biryani is a royal Pakistani rice dish cooked using the ancient dum cooking technique where marinated meat and partially cooked basmati rice are layered in a large pot, sealed with dough, and slow cooked over low heat. The steam trapped inside infuses the rice with rich flavors of saffron, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and caramelized onions. This method of cooking originated in the Mughal royal kitchens and is considered the most aromatic and flavorful biryani style. It is typically made with beef or mutton and is halal.

Sheer Khurma is a traditional Pakistani and South Asian dessert prepared especially during Eid celebrations. It is a rich vermicelli pudding made by cooking thin roasted vermicelli noodles in full fat milk with dates, dried fruits such as almonds, pistachios, cashews, and raisins, sweetened with sugar and flavored with cardamom and rose water. The dish is creamy, fragrant, and deeply satisfying. It holds strong religious and cultural significance as it is the first food consumed after Eid prayers and symbolizes celebration, sweetness, and family togetherness. It is vegetarian.

Salmon is one of the most nutritious fish available and is widely consumed as a healthy food globally. It is an excellent source of omega-3 fatty acids which are essential for heart health, brain function, and reducing inflammation in the body. A 100 gram serving of salmon provides approximately 25 grams of high quality protein, vitamin D, vitamin B12, selenium, and potassium. It can be grilled, baked, poached, or eaten raw as sashimi. Salmon is gluten-free, dairy-free, and suitable for pescatarian diets. Regular consumption is associated with reduced risk of heart disease and improved cognitive health.

Quinoa is a highly nutritious ancient grain originally cultivated by the Inca civilization in the Andes mountains of South America. Unlike most plant foods, quinoa is a complete protein containing all nine essential amino acids, making it exceptionally valuable for vegetarians and vegans. A single cup of cooked quinoa provides 8 grams of protein, 5 grams of dietary fiber, magnesium, iron, zinc, and folate. It is naturally gluten-free, low glycemic index, and suitable for people with celiac disease. Quinoa can be used as a substitute for rice, added to salads, or made into porridge.

Avocado is a creamy, nutrient-dense fruit native to Central America that has become one of the most popular health foods worldwide. It is exceptionally rich in heart-healthy monounsaturated fats, particularly oleic acid which is the same fatty acid found in olive oil. Avocados are an excellent source of potassium, containing more than bananas, as well as folate, vitamin K, vitamin C, and dietary fiber. They are naturally cholesterol-free and sodium-free. Avocados are vegan, gluten-free, and paleo-friendly. They are commonly eaten on toast, in salads, as guacamole, or blended into smoothies.

Greek Yogurt is a thick, strained yogurt that has been concentrated by removing excess whey, resulting in a creamy texture with significantly higher protein content than regular yogurt. A single cup of plain Greek yogurt provides up to 20 grams of protein, making it one of the best high-protein breakfast options available. It is also rich in probiotics which support gut health and immunity, as well as calcium, vitamin B12, and phosphorus. Greek yogurt is suitable for vegetarians and those following a low-carb or high-protein diet. It can be eaten with fruits, used as a substitute for sour cream, or blended into smoothies.

Lentil Soup is a highly nutritious and affordable healthy meal consumed across the Middle East, South Asia, and Mediterranean regions. Lentils are one of the best plant-based sources of protein and fiber, with one cup of cooked lentils providing 18 grams of protein and 16 grams of dietary fiber. The soup is typically prepared by simmering red or green lentils with onions, garlic, cumin, turmeric, and lemon juice. Lentils are rich in iron, folate, and manganese. This dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and very low in fat, making it suitable for heart-healthy and diabetic-friendly diets.

Margherita Pizza is the classic Italian pizza originating from Naples, Italy, considered the foundation of all modern pizzas. It was allegedly created in 1889 in honor of Queen Margherita of Italy and features the three colors of the Italian flag. It is made with a hand-stretched thin wheat dough base, topped with San Marzano tomato sauce, fresh buffalo mozzarella cheese, and fresh basil leaves, then baked at extremely high temperature in a wood-fired stone oven. The result is a crispy charred crust with a soft, chewy interior. Authentic Neapolitan Margherita Pizza is protected by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage.

Tacos al Pastor is an iconic Mexican street food dish with roots in Lebanese shawarma brought to Mexico by Lebanese immigrants in the early 20th century. Thin slices of marinated pork are stacked on a vertical spit called a trompo, grilled slowly, and shaved off when cooked. The pork is marinated in dried chilies, achiote paste, pineapple, garlic, and cumin. It is served on small corn tortillas topped with fresh pineapple, chopped onion, coriander, and salsa verde. Tacos al Pastor is one of the most popular and beloved street foods in Mexico City and represents the fusion of Mexican and Middle Eastern culinary traditions.

Croissant is a buttery, flaky, crescent-shaped French pastry made using a laminated dough technique where layers of butter are folded into the dough repeatedly to create hundreds of thin, airy layers. The croissant originated in Austria as a kipferl but was perfected in France where it became a symbol of French bakery culture. A traditional croissant requires at least two days of preparation including resting and chilling the dough between folds. It is typically eaten as a breakfast pastry with jam, butter, or plain alongside coffee. Croissants are a vegetarian food and are a staple of French cafes and bakeries worldwide.

Jollof Rice is a West African one-pot rice dish that is arguably the most popular and debated dish across the African continent. It is made by cooking long grain rice in a rich, spiced tomato and pepper base along with onions, garlic, scotch bonnet chilies, and various seasonings. The rice absorbs all the flavors and develops a distinctive smoky taste when cooked in the traditional way over an open fire. Jollof Rice is commonly served at celebrations, parties, and family gatherings throughout Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, and other West African countries. It is typically accompanied by fried plantains, coleslaw, and grilled chicken. It is naturally gluten-free.

Paella is Spain's most internationally recognized dish, originating from the Valencia region on the eastern coast of Spain. It is a large, shallow pan rice dish cooked over an open fire and characterized by its distinctive golden color from saffron spice. Traditional Valencian paella is made with short grain bomba rice, chicken, rabbit, green beans, butter beans, tomato, and rosemary. Seafood paella features prawns, mussels, squid, and clams. The rice develops a crispy caramelized bottom layer called socarrat which is considered the most prized part of the dish. Paella is gluten-free and is cooked using the absorption method where rice soaks up a rich homemade stock.

Tom Kha Gai is a traditional Thai coconut milk soup made with tender chicken, galangal, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, mushrooms, and Thai chilies. The soup has a rich, creamy texture from the coconut milk balanced with the sourness of lime juice and fish sauce. It is one of Thailand's most beloved comfort soups, served in homes and restaurants across the country. Tom Kha Gai is gluten-free, dairy-free, and high in healthy fats from coconut milk. It is typically garnished with fresh coriander and red chilies.

Massaman Curry is a rich and mildly spiced Thai curry with strong influences from Persian and Indian cuisines, brought to Thailand by Muslim traders centuries ago. It is made with beef or chicken slow-cooked in a thick coconut milk sauce with potatoes, onions, peanuts, cardamom, cinnamon, and tamarind. The flavor is uniquely sweet, savory, and slightly sour. Massaman Curry is one of the most complex curries in Thai cuisine and was once voted the most delicious food in the world. It is gluten-free and halal when made with beef or chicken.

Greek Salad known as Horiatiki is a classic Mediterranean dish made with ripe tomatoes, cucumbers, red onions, green bell peppers, Kalamata olives, and a large slab of feta cheese, dressed with extra virgin olive oil and dried oregano. It contains no lettuce, which distinguishes it from other salads. Greek Salad is extremely nutritious, rich in vitamins C and K, antioxidants, and heart-healthy monounsaturated fats from olive oil. It is naturally vegetarian, gluten-free, and low in calories. It represents the simplicity and freshness of Mediterranean cuisine.

Shakshuka is a popular Middle Eastern and North African dish of poached eggs in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce. It originated in Tunisia and became extremely popular across Israel, Egypt, and the entire Middle East. The sauce is made by simmering tomatoes, red peppers, onions, garlic, cumin, paprika, and cayenne pepper together, then cracking eggs directly into the sauce to poach. Shakshuka is nutritious, high in protein from eggs, and rich in lycopene from tomatoes. It is vegetarian, gluten-free, and typically served with crusty bread for dipping.

Moussaka is a classic Greek comfort food dish made with layers of fried eggplant, spiced minced lamb or beef cooked with tomatoes and cinnamon, and a thick creamy bechamel sauce on top, baked until golden brown. It is the Greek equivalent of lasagna and is deeply satisfying and filling. Moussaka has strong cultural significance in Greece and is served at family gatherings and celebrations. It is rich in protein and fiber from the eggplant and meat. A vegetarian version replaces meat with lentils. It contains gluten from the bechamel sauce.

Foul Medames is one of the oldest and most widely consumed dishes in the Middle East and North Africa, dating back to ancient Egypt. It is made from slow-cooked fava beans seasoned with olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, and parsley. It is the national breakfast dish of Egypt and is also popular in Sudan, Lebanon, and Syria. Foul Medames is extremely nutritious, being an excellent source of plant-based protein with 13 grams per cup, dietary fiber, iron, and folate. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and very affordable.

Broccoli is one of the most nutritious vegetables available and is widely recognized as a superfood due to its exceptional health benefits. It belongs to the cruciferous vegetable family and is packed with vitamin C, vitamin K, folate, potassium, and fiber. A single cup of broccoli contains more vitamin C than an orange. Broccoli contains sulforaphane, a powerful compound with anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties. It can be steamed, roasted, stir-fried, or eaten raw. Broccoli is vegan, gluten-free, low in calories with only 55 calories per cup, and suitable for all dietary lifestyles.

Sweet Potato is a highly nutritious root vegetable that is one of the best sources of beta-carotene, which the body converts into vitamin A essential for eye health and immune function. A single medium sweet potato provides over 400 percent of the daily recommended intake of vitamin A. Sweet potatoes are also rich in vitamin C, manganese, potassium, and dietary fiber. They have a naturally sweet flavor and can be baked, mashed, roasted, or made into soups. Sweet potatoes are vegan, gluten-free, low glycemic index, and anti-inflammatory, making them ideal for diabetic-friendly diets.

Chia Seed Pudding is a modern health food made by soaking chia seeds in milk or plant-based milk overnight until they expand and form a thick, creamy pudding-like texture. Chia seeds are among the most nutrient-dense foods on the planet, containing 11 grams of fiber, 4 grams of protein, and significant amounts of omega-3 fatty acids, calcium, magnesium, and phosphorus per two tablespoons. The pudding is typically sweetened with honey or maple syrup and topped with fresh fruits and nuts. It is gluten-free, can be made vegan with plant milk, and is an excellent high-fiber breakfast option.

Edamame are young green soybeans harvested before they fully ripen, popular in Japanese and Chinese cuisine as a healthy snack. They are boiled or steamed in their pods and lightly salted before serving. Edamame is one of the few plant foods that is a complete protein, containing all essential amino acids. A half cup serving provides 9 grams of protein, 4 grams of fiber, and significant amounts of vitamin K, folate, and manganese. They are naturally vegan, gluten-free, low in calories, and make an excellent protein-rich snack for athletes and vegetarians.

Kale is considered one of the most nutrient-dense foods on Earth and has earned its reputation as a superfood. A single cup of raw kale contains vitamins A, C, and K exceeding the daily recommended values, along with calcium, potassium, magnesium, and powerful antioxidants including quercetin and kaempferol. Kale belongs to the cruciferous vegetable family and has strong anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. It can be eaten raw in salads, sauteed with garlic, blended into smoothies, or baked into crispy kale chips. Kale is vegan, gluten-free, and extremely low in calories.

Mac and Cheese is the ultimate American comfort food that has been beloved by children and adults across the United States for generations. It consists of tender elbow macaroni pasta coated in a rich, creamy cheese sauce typically made from cheddar cheese, butter, flour, and milk. The dish originated in Europe but became deeply embedded in American food culture. It can be made on the stovetop or baked in the oven with a crispy breadcrumb topping. Mac and Cheese is a vegetarian dish, high in calcium and protein, and is one of the most consumed comfort foods in North America.

Ramen in its modern Japanese form is one of the world's greatest comfort foods, consisting of wheat noodles served in a deeply flavored broth that has been simmered for up to 18 hours with pork bones, chicken, vegetables, and aromatics. Toppings include chashu pork belly, soft boiled soy marinated eggs, bamboo shoots, nori seaweed, and green onions. There are four main regional styles including Tonkotsu from Fukuoka, Shoyu from Tokyo, Miso from Sapporo, and Shio from Hakodate. Each style has distinct flavors and cultural significance. Ramen culture in Japan is taken extremely seriously with dedicated ramen restaurants called ramen-ya.

Poutine is Canada's most iconic comfort food originating from Quebec in the 1950s. It consists of crispy french fries topped with fresh cheese curds and smothered in hot brown gravy. The heat from the gravy slightly melts the cheese curds creating an irresistibly gooey, salty, and savory dish. Poutine is deeply embedded in Canadian culture and national identity. It is served in fast food chains, fine dining restaurants, and food trucks across Canada. Modern versions include toppings like pulled pork, lobster, foie gras, and smoked meat. It is vegetarian in its classic form.

Pierogi are traditional Polish dumplings that are one of the most beloved comfort foods in Eastern Europe. They are made from unleavened dough stuffed with various fillings including mashed potatoes and cheese, sauerkraut and mushrooms, ground meat, or sweet fillings like blueberries and cherries. They are boiled first then pan fried in butter until golden and crispy, served with sour cream and caramelized onions. Pierogi have deep cultural significance in Poland and are served at Christmas Eve dinner and family celebrations. They are vegetarian when filled with potato and cheese, and represent centuries of Polish culinary tradition.

Beef Stroganoff is a classic Russian comfort food dish that originated in 19th century Russia and became popular worldwide. It consists of tender strips of beef sauteed with mushrooms and onions in a rich sour cream sauce, served over egg noodles or rice. The dish was named after the wealthy Stroganov family and became internationally famous after spreading through Europe and America. Beef Stroganoff is high in protein from the beef and provides calcium from sour cream. It is warm, filling, and deeply satisfying especially during cold weather. Modern variations include chicken and mushroom versions.

Tagine is a slow-cooked North African stew named after the distinctive conical clay pot it is cooked in. Moroccan Tagine is one of the most complex and aromatic dishes in the world, combining meat such as lamb or chicken with preserved lemons, olives, dried fruits like apricots and prunes, honey, and a blend of warming spices including ras el hanout, saffron, ginger, and cinnamon. The conical lid creates a self-basting cooking environment that keeps meat incredibly tender and moist. Tagine is served with couscous or flatbread and represents the rich culinary heritage of Morocco. It is gluten-free and halal.

Injera with Doro Wat is Ethiopia's national dish and represents the heart of Ethiopian cuisine and culture. Injera is a large spongy sourdough flatbread made from teff flour that serves as both plate and utensil. Doro Wat is a deeply spiced chicken stew made with berbere spice paste, niter kibbeh spiced butter, onions, garlic, ginger, and hard boiled eggs. The stew is poured over the injera and eaten by tearing pieces of injera to scoop up the stew. Teff is extremely nutritious, being high in protein, fiber, iron, and calcium. The dish is naturally gluten-free when made with pure teff.

Ceviche is a popular Latin American dish considered the national dish of Peru, made from fresh raw fish cured in citrus juices primarily lime, mixed with sliced onions, cilantro, and aji amarillo chili peppers. The acid in the lime juice chemically denatures the proteins in the fish creating a texture similar to cooking without heat. Peruvian ceviche is served with corn, sweet potato, and lettuce. It is extremely fresh, light, and healthy being high in protein, omega-3 fatty acids, and vitamin C. Ceviche is gluten-free, dairy-free, and low in calories. It represents the coastal culinary identity of Peru.

Butter Mochi is a beloved Hawaiian comfort food dessert that combines Japanese mochi rice cake tradition with American butter cake culture. It is made from glutinous rice flour mixed with butter, sugar, eggs, coconut milk, and vanilla, baked in a pan until the edges are slightly crispy while the inside remains chewy and dense. Butter Mochi has a unique texture that is unlike any other dessert, being simultaneously chewy, soft, and slightly sticky. It is extremely popular at Hawaiian potlucks, school fundraisers, and family gatherings. It is gluten-free as it uses rice flour instead of wheat flour.
